Fix memory leak in event listener cleanup #42

Open
octocat 2024-01-15

When calling removeEventListener inside a closure, the listener reference is not properly tracked. This causes a memory leak over time, especially in long-running applications.

Steps to reproduce:

  1. Create an event listener with an anonymous function
  2. Attempt to remove it later
  3. Observe that memory usage increases

Expected: listener is cleaned up. Actual: listener remains attached.

bug memory performance
monalisa 2024-01-16

I can reproduce this. The fix would be to store a reference to the bound function:

const boundHandler = handler.bind(this);
element.addEventListener('click', boundHandler);
// later:
element.removeEventListener('click', boundHandler);
hubot 2024-01-17

PR #43 has been submitted to fix this issue.
